content.problemStatement:
For an integer KKK and a permutation A=[A1,A2,‚Ä¶,AN]A = [A_1, A_2, \dots, A_N]A=[A1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã] of values from 111 to NNN, we define a function f(A)f(A)f(A) that returns the number of pairs of integers (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that


1‚â§i<j‚â§N1 \le i \lt j \le N1‚â§i<j‚â§N
j>i+Kj \gt i+Kj>i+K
Aj>AiA_j \gt A_iAj‚Äã>Ai‚Äã
For all x‚â•(i+1)x \geq (i+1)x‚â•(i+1) and x‚â§(j‚àí1)x\leq(j-1)x‚â§(j‚àí1) , Ax<AiA_x \lt A_iAx‚Äã<Ai‚Äã
for all x<ix \lt ix<i , Ax<AiA_x \lt A_iAx‚Äã<Ai‚Äã
In simple words, in the subarray A[1..j]A_{[1..j]}A[1..j]‚Äã, AiA_iAi‚Äã is the second-largest element and AjA_jAj‚Äã is the
largest element.

You are given KKK. Calculate ‚àëf(A)\sum f(A)‚àëf(A) for ALL permutations AAA of size NNN.

Since the answer can be very large, find the sum over modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of T test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK.


Output Format
For each test case, output a single line containing one integer - the sum of f(P)f(P)f(P) for
all permutation of size NNN for the given KKK.
constraints: **Subtask 2 (30 points):** Sum of N over all test cases does not exceed 10710^7107.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111:

f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0 for permutations {1,2,31,2,31,2,3}, {1,3,21,3,21,3,2}, {2,3,12,3,12,3,1}, {3,1,23,1,23,1,2}, {3,2,13,2,13,2,1} .
f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1 for permutation {2,1,32,1,32,1,3}.



Therefore ‚àëf(P)=1\sum f(P) = 1‚àëf(P)=1.


Test case 222:

f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1 for permutations {3,1,2,43,1,2,43,1,2,4} and {3,2,1,43,2,1,43,2,1,4}.
f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0 for all other permutations.



Therefore ‚àëf(A)=2\sum f(A) = 2‚àëf(A)=2.
